title: Sepulchral
content:
Swifter than aught 'neath the sun the car of Simonides moved him.
Two things he could not out-run Death and a Woman who loved him.
meaning:
"Sepulchral" is a quintessential example of Rudyard Kipling's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
